title: TopologicalOrder

tex_statement.raw:
% Definition file for row def_3_8 -- "TopologicalOrder".
% Auto-generated stub by scaffold/solve_chapter.py at row start. The
% manager agent (or a worker dispatched by it) fills the body below with the
% Lean-faithful definition statement(s). Stays close to the lecture notes.
%
% This file is a sibling of `main.tex` inside `<subsection>/tex/`. The
% `subfiles` package lets it compile both standalone (resolving its
% preamble via `main.tex`) and as part of `main.tex` via `\subfile{...}`.

\documentclass[main]{subfiles}

\begin{document}
% ref: def_3_8
% title: TopologicalOrder
\def\rowref{def\_3\_8}
\phantomsection\label{def_3_8}

\begin{Def}[Topological order]
    Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG.
    A \emph{topological order} of $G$ is a total order $<$ of $J \cup V$ such that for all $v,w \in G$:
    \[ v \in \Pa^G(w) \; \implies \; v < w.\]
    Equivalently, it can be described as an indexing of the nodes $J \cup V = \{v_1,\dots,v_K\}$ where parents always precede their children.
\end{Def}

\end{document}
tex_statement.html: Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG. A topological order of $G$ is a total order $<$ of $J \cup V$ such that for all $v,w \in G$: \[ v \in \Pa^G(w) \; \implies \; v < w.\] Equivalently, it can be described as an indexing of the nodes $J \cup V = \{v_1,\dots,v_K\}$ where parents always precede their children.

design_choices:
## Design choices

**Predicate, not bundled structure.** The formalisation exposes `IsTopologicalOrder G lt` as a `Prop` parametric in the relation `lt : Node Ôćĺ Node Ôćĺ Prop`, rather than a record bundling the relation together with its order axioms and the parents-precede-children clause. Every downstream consumer ÔÇö `def_3_9`'s `Pred^G_<`, the intervention/marginalisation/acyclification preservation lemmas, and the chapter-8 iSCM solution-uniqueness argument ÔÇö takes `<` as a free parameter, so a structure form would force a `.lt`-field projection at every call site. The existential `Ôłâ lt, G.IsTopologicalOrder lt` recovers the LN's noun phrasing "a topological order of $G$" without loss of expressivity.

**Carrier-restricted axioms on the ambient `Node` type.** The relation is `Node Ôćĺ Node Ôćĺ Prop` with irreflexivity, transitivity and trichotomy each guarded by `v Ôłł G`, rather than a `LinearOrder` instance on the subtype `{v // v Ôłł G.J Ôł¬ G.V}`. This avoids subtype coercions at every `Pa`-lookup or walk-step, keeps composition with `def_3_5`'s `Finset Node`-valued family relations friction-free, and sidesteps the inhabitedness gotchas of Mathlib's `LinearOrder` on a possibly-empty subtype. Out-of-carrier values of `lt` are vacuous data, parallel to how `def_3_5` treats out-of-graph vertices.

**Helper `IsTotalOrderOn` factors the surface "such that" split.** The LN reads "a total order $<$ of $J \cup V$ *such that* parents precede children". The helper isolates the first clause as its own predicate so that `def_3_9` ÔÇö whose hypothesis is literally "let $<$ be a total order of $J \cup V$" ÔÇö can reuse it directly without inlining three conjuncts twice. It is marked as a statement-support helper so the website builder pulls it alongside the main statement.

**Three-axiom total order.** Trichotomy is spelled `lt v w ÔłĘ v = w ÔłĘ lt w v` (mirroring Mathlib's `IsTrichotomous`); asymmetry and exclusivity of the disjuncts are derivable from irreflexivity + transitivity and so are not separately stated. The "connex" alternative would force a `v Ôëá w` hypothesis at every use site.

**Instance-implicit binders.** Vertices in the transitivity, trichotomy and parents-precede clauses use `ÔŽâ ÔŽä` so consumers can write e.g. `htop.2 hPa` without naming `v` and `w`; elaboration is deferred to the call site, which is more robust under `simp` and `rcases` than regular `{}`.

**Membership preconditions only where needed.** The parents-precede clause is stated as `ÔłÇ ÔŽâv wÔŽä, v Ôłł G.Pa w Ôćĺ lt v w` ÔÇö no separate `v Ôłł G`, `w Ôłł G` hypotheses, since `v Ôłł G.Pa w` already implies both via the carrier discipline of `def_3_5` and `G.hE_subset`. The `IsTotalOrderOn` axioms, by contrast, quantify over arbitrary `lt`-related pairs and therefore must carry explicit membership.

**Strict `<`, no `Decidable` instance, no separate indexing form.** The relation is strict, matching the LN; a reflexive variant is the one-line `fun v w => lt v w ÔłĘ v = w`. `DecidableRel lt` is left to the consumer to avoid over-constraining classical existence statements. The LN's equivalent "indexing" reformulation $\{v_1,\dots,v_K\}$ is treated as a downstream theorem (recoverable via `Finset.sort`) rather than a second primary definition, so that the relation form remains the single source of truth.
